title: Building an Agent Loop
tagline: The core of turbo-agent-kit, no framework, ~300 lines
interviewPrompt: build the core of an LLM agent loop from scratch, no framework

beats.interviewer: For today's software engineering interview: build the core of an LLM agent loop from scratch, no framework. Start with the shape.
beats.interviewee: Sure. At its core an agent loop is a conversation plus a controller that decides when to stop. I just merged this in turbo-agent-kit, about three hundred lines. Let me build it up.
beats.show: An agent loop is a conversation plus a controller that decides when to stop.

beats.interviewer: What's the data model underneath it?
beats.interviewee: Everything is a list of messages: user, assistant, tool. A user message goes in, and the orchestrator owns the turn from there.
beats.show: One conversation is an ordered list of messages: user, assistant, tool.

beats.interviewer: How does the model actually decide to use a tool?
beats.interviewee: Each step I hand the whole message history to the model and ask one thing: give me a final answer, or a tool call.
beats.show: Every step asks the model: final text, or call a tool?

beats.interviewer: Say it asks for a tool. What happens then?
beats.interviewee: The name is looked up in a registry and the arguments are validated with zod before a single line runs.
beats.show: Tool calls resolve by name in a registry; args are zod-validated first.

beats.interviewer: And you feed the result back into the model?
beats.interviewee: Right. The tool output gets pushed into the conversation and the loop runs again. It is capped at five steps so it can never spin forever.
beats.show: Tool output feeds back in; the loop repeats, capped at 5 steps.

beats.interviewer: Anything you would add before we wrap?
beats.interviewee: Two things I bolted on. Telemetry times every model and tool call, and an onMessage hook streams each message so a UI can show the turn as it happens.
beats.show: Telemetry times every call; onMessage streams the turn live.
